title: YouTrack Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**YouTrack** is JetBrains' issue-tracking and agile project-management product. The REST API lives on a per-tenant cluster: Cloud at `https://{instance}.youtrack.cloud/api`, self-hosted at `https://youtrack.example.com/api`. This connector — `YouTrackConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "youtrack"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users` + `/users/me` | Current user, list, get |
| Projects | `/admin/projects` | List, get |
| Issues | `/issues` | List, get, create, update, delete |
| Comments | `/issues/{id}/comments` | List, add |
| Tags | `/issueTags` | List |
| Time Tracking | `/issues/{id}/timeTracking/workItems` | List logged work items |
| Agile Boards | `/agiles` | List boards |
| Sprints | `/agiles/{agileId}/sprints` | List sprints on a board |
| Articles | `/articles` | Knowledge-base articles |

The connector normalises issues into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::YouTrackHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a YouTrack permanent token

1. Sign in to your YouTrack instance (`https://{instance}.youtrack.cloud` or your self-hosted URL).
2. Open **Profile → Account Security → Permanent Tokens**.
3. Click **New token**, give it a name + scope (typically `YouTrack` service with read+write on the surfaces you need: Issues, Projects, Articles, Comments).
4. Copy the token — it starts with `perm:` and is shown only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → YouTrack**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `base_url` (text) | Instance root URL — e.g. `https://yourorg.youtrack.cloud` (the connector appends `/api` automatically). |
| `permanent_token` (secret) | Permanent token from step 4 |
| `default_project_id` (text, optional) | Project short-name or internal id (e.g. `0-1`). Used by `sync()` and as a fallback for `create_issue`. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 200 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `base_url` + `permanent_token` are present, normalises the base URL (`https://{host}` or `https://{host}/api` are both accepted), initialises the `YouTrackHTTPClient`, and probes `GET /users/me?fields=login` to verify the token.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which re-runs the `/users/me` probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On network failure → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
YouTrack REST API uses **server-to-server permanent-token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no refresh, no expiry — but admins can revoke tokens manually.

### Credential model

- `permanent_token` is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <perm:...>`. Tokens always start with the `perm:` prefix.
- `base_url` is per-tenant. Cloud: `https://{instance}.youtrack.cloud`. Self-hosted: `https://youtrack.example.com`. The connector calls `normalize_base_url(...)` and ends every request URL with `/api`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `base_url` + `permanent_token`. Initialises `YouTrackHTTPClient`. Calls `GET /users/me?fields=login` as a token probe. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured permanent token; there is no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me?fields=login` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the permanent token lives in `config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <perm:...>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | text | yes | `self.base_url` | Per-tenant YouTrack root; normalised to `{host}/api` by `helpers/utils.py::normalize_base_url`. |
| `permanent_token` | secret | yes | `self.permanent_token` | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header value. |
| `default_project_id` | text | no | `self.default_project_id` | Filter for `sync()` and fallback for `create_issue`. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 200) | Client-side soft cap. |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["base_url", "permanent_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

There is no provider-wide host constant — `base_url` is always tenant-supplied.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `YouTrackConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::YouTrackHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `base_url` + `permanent_token`; initialise `YouTrackHTTPClient`; probe `GET /users/me?fields=login`. Returns `(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)` on success.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured permanent token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /users/me?fields=login`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/issues` (filtered by `default_project_id` and `since` when set), normalises each into a `NormalizedDocument`, calls `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

### `async get_issue_normalized(issue_id) -> NormalizedDocument`
Fetches one issue with the full field set and returns it normalised.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /users/me`.

### `async list_users(query=None, skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /users` with `$skip` / `$top` paging.

### `async get_user(user_id, fields=...) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{user_id}`.

children.title: Projects
children.content:
### `async list_projects(skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /admin/projects`.

### `async get_project(project_id, fields=None) -> Dict`
`GET /admin/projects/{project_id}`.

children.title: Issues
children.content:
### `async list_issues(query=None, skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /issues` — `query` accepts the YouTrack query language (e.g. `project: ACME #Unresolved`).

### `async get_issue(issue_id, fields=None) -> Dict`
`GET /issues/{issue_id}` — accepts internal id (`2-15`) or readable id (`ACME-15`).

### `async create_issue(project_id, summary, description='', custom_fields=None, fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /issues` — body shape: `{"project": {"id": project_id}, "summary": ..., "description": ..., "customFields": [...]}`. Falls back to `default_project_id` when `project_id` is blank.

### `async update_issue(issue_id, summary=None, description=None, custom_fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST /issues/{issue_id}` — only the supplied fields are sent.

### `async delete_issue(issue_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /issues/{issue_id}`.

children.title: Comments · Tags · Time Tracking
children.content:
### `async add_comment(issue_id, text) -> Dict`
`POST /issues/{issue_id}/comments` — body `{"text": ...}`.

### `async list_comments(issue_id, skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /issues/{issue_id}/comments`.

### `async list_tags(skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /issueTags` — account-wide tags.

### `async list_time_tracking(issue_id, skip=0, top=100) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /issues/{issue_id}/timeTracking/workItems`.

children.title: Agile Boards · Sprints · Articles
children.content:
### `async list_boards(skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /agiles` — agile boards visible to the token.

### `async list_sprints(board_id, skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /agiles/{board_id}/sprints`.

### `async list_articles(query=None, skip=0, top=100, fields=...) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /articles` — knowledge-base articles.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
YouTrackError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── YouTrackAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── YouTrackBadRequestError            # 400
├── YouTrackNotFoundError              # 404
├── YouTrackConflictError              # 409
├── YouTrackPreconditionError          # 428 (revision mismatch)
├── YouTrackRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── YouTrackServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
YouTrackNetworkError = YouTrackServerError   # legacy name
YouTrackNotFound     = YouTrackNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 428 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt` + jitter, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=16s`, retry up to `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` / connect/read errors | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `YouTrackNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `base_url` or `permanent_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Tokens always start with `perm:`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401/403 from `/users/me` — token revoked or wrong base_url | Generate a fresh permanent token in YouTrack → Profile → Account Security → Permanent Tokens. Verify the base_url matches the instance the token was issued for. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, PENDING)` at install | Network error reaching the YouTrack host | Check `base_url` host is reachable from the gateway (DNS, firewall, VPN). |
| `youtrack.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (YouTrack per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_issue` | `issue_id` is wrong, or the token's user lacks read permission on that project | Try the readable id (e.g. `ACME-15`) or the internal id (e.g. `2-15`). Verify project membership. |
| `create_issue` raises `ValueError: project_id is required` | No `project_id` supplied AND `default_project_id` not set | Pass `project_id` explicitly OR set `default_project_id` in the install fields. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'YouTrackNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `YouTrackNetworkError = YouTrackServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| `from shared.base_connector import ...` fails outside the harness | Connector root or core/ not on `sys.path` | The package `__init__.py` self-bootstraps both. If you import the package via `from connector import ...` directly, ensure `PYTHONPATH` includes both the connector root and the core/ directory. |
